title: HOMEPLATE - 211info
text: HOMEPLATE HOMEPLATE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a weekly drop-in center where youth can access food, clothing, showers, and hygiene products and receive referrals for other resources. Crafts, sports, games, and other activities may also be held at the drop-in centers. Outreach workers are also available to meet youth anywhere in Washington County to discuss resources. Operates education and employment programs with one-on-one support for job search, application assistance, skills training, resume and cover letter creation, interview coaching, school reconnection, navigating financial aid, enrolling in college or trade schools, and life skills. 12685 SW 4th Street Beaverton, OR 97005 Eligibility: Youth ages 12-24 who are experiencing homelessness or at risk of homelessness. Youth may access services until their 25th birthday. Hours: Monday/Wednesday 3pm-5pm and 6pm-8pm, Tuesday 2pm-5pm Email (503) 608-7252 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (503) 608-7252 Main phone Community Line Main phone (503) 608-7252 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Walk in. Youth are asked to provide their name and age only at the drop-in center. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Washington County TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Corner of SW 3rd and Watson. TriMet Max blue and red lines and bus lines 20, 52, 53, 54, 57, 58, 61, 76, 78, and 88 all stop at Beaverton Transit Center. From there, cross over SW Lombard Avenue until SW Watson and turn left onto Watson Avenue until 3rd Avenue. Bus lines 57, 76, and 78 also drop off closer to the location. Services: Community Meals * Homeless Youth General Recreational Activities/Sports * Homeless Youth Homeless Drop In Centers * Youth Issues Job Finding Assistance Personal/Grooming Supplies * Homeless Youth Prejob Guidance Specialized Information and Referral * Homelessness Issues Street Outreach Programs * Homeless Youth If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. HOMEPLATE
